From SEO to AEO: Structuring Your Site So AI and Humans Choose Your Firm

Introduction SEO as we knew it is evolving — and fast. With Google’s AI Overviews and the rise of AI-driven search assistants, the new frontier for law firms is

## Introduction SEO as we knew it is evolving — and fast. With Google’s AI Overviews and the rise of AI-driven search assistants, the new frontier for law firms is **Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)**. Instead of optimizing only for rankings, your firm must now optimize for **answers** — concise, credible, structured responses that AI systems trust. This guide explains how to transition from traditional SEO to AEO in 2025, structuring your site so both AI and humans see you as the authoritative choice. ## Why Law Firms Must Think Beyond SEO AI systems like Google’s AI Overviews, SearchGPT, and Perplexity are reshaping how people discover legal information. Clients now ask conversational questions such as:
  • “Can a non-compete be enforced in Florida?”
  • “How much does a business lawyer cost near me?”
    • AI tools then generate summary answers, citing only a handful of trusted sources. Your law firm’s visibility depends less on keywords and more on **credibility, structure, and corroboration**. Firms with strong entity clarity, schema, and experience signals will dominate these AI-driven results. ## The AEO Framework for Law Firms ### 1. Build Clear Entities AI models think in terms of entities — not just text. Your site should clearly define:
  • **Organization:** Firm name, address, consistent NAP
  • **Attorneys:** Bios, bar numbers, specialties, schema
  • **Jurisdictions:** States, counties, or courts you serve
  • **Practice Areas:** Structured, intent-based pages (e.g., “Business Litigation in Tampa”)
    • ### 2. Structure Your Content Like an Answer AI Overviews pull concise, authoritative answers. Use this structure:
  • Provide the answer in 1–2 sentences.
  • Add 2–3 sentences of context.
  • Cite reputable sources or pillar pages.
  • Include a “When to Contact an Attorney” CTA.
    • Example: Instead of “Florida Non-Compete Laws Explained,” use: **“Are non-competes enforceable in Florida? It depends on scope and duration.”****** Then explain and cite Florida Statute §542.335. ### 3. Use Schema Everywhere Add schema such as:
  • LegalService
  • Attorney
  • Organization
  • FAQ
    • Schema helps AI confirm accuracy and authorship. ### 4. Optimize for E-E-A-T To strengthen experience and authority signals:
  • Add bar numbers, admissions, and years in practice
  • Link to reviews, awards, and media
  • Use attorney bylines
  • Include About and bio links across posts
    • ### 5. Integrate AEO With Intake Visibility must connect to revenue. Integrate your CRM, call tracking, and analytics so you can measure AEO’s real impact on consults and retained cases. ## Designing a Site Architecture AI Understands Your content structure should resemble a semantic knowledge graph:
  • **Homepage:** Define your main entity
  • **Pillar Pages:** One for each practice + jurisdiction
  • **FAQ clusters:** Short, question-based posts
  • **Attorney bios:** With schema and credentials
  • **Blog hub:** Posts that reinforce main entities
    • Use internal links strategically across service pages. ## Tracking AEO Success AEO uses KPIs beyond impressions:
  • AI Overview visibility and inclusion rates
  • Consult-set rate from AEO-sourced leads
  • Retention rate
  • Revenue per AEO-qualified consult
    • Combining GA4, CRM, and call tracking gives a complete picture. ## Law Firm AEO Optimization Checklist
  • Publish concise, answer-based content
  • Maintain consistent NAP and organization entity
  • Add LegalService + Attorney schema
  • Include bar numbers and bylines
  • Build internal linking from FAQ → Pillar → Practice → Contact
  • Track AEO KPIs
    • ## Ready to Optimize for AEO? Your next clients are asking AI assistants for answers — make sure they find yours. Schedule an AEO audit with Hughey LLC to evaluate your schema, content, and intake performance. Book a discovery call ## FAQs
    What is Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)? AEO is the process of structuring your content so AI systems can extract, trust, and cite your answers in AI-generated search results.
    How is AEO different from SEO? SEO focuses on rankings. AEO focuses on being **the answer** AI tools select.
    Does AEO replace SEO? No. AEO enhances SEO by adding structured data, clear entities, and concise answers.
    What schema should law firms use? Organization, LegalService, Attorney, FAQ, HowTo, and Review schema.
    AEO is the process of structuring your content so AI systems can extract, trust, and cite your answers in AI-generated search results. SEO focuses on rankings. AEO focuses on being **the answer** AI tools select. No. AEO enhances SEO by adding structured data, clear entities, and concise answers. Organization, LegalService, Attorney, FAQ, HowTo, and Review schema.

    About the Author

    Joe Hughey is the founder of Hughey LLC, a law firm marketing strategy consulting firm. With 20+ years of legal marketing experience, Joe works exclusively with law firms to build marketing operations that generate retained clients.
